summary: The input must be inspected and the XOR domain is contractually fixed at X = 1024; frequency-compressed residue-class DP performs O(nX) work, which is linear in n under the complete legal domain.

optimality.argument: Changing any one array value can change its residue-class frequency and the optimum, so an exact algorithm must inspect all n inputs. Every legal value is ten-bit, fixing the complete XOR state domain at X = 1024 rather than allowing it to grow with n. The DP crosses each observed group value with X states; the total number of observed distinct values across groups is at most n, giving O(nX) = O(n) time over the legal contract.

replacement_checks.evidence: Any uninspected element may be the occurrence that changes a residue class's best retained value and therefore the minimum number of edits.
Counting groups is O(n); baseline initialization is O(kX); and transitions total O(X times the sum of group distinct counts), at most O(nX), with X fixed at 1024.
Authored fixtures cover k = 1, k = n, zero required changes, one required change, periodic classes, mixed group frequencies, a singleton, and the maximum legal value 1023.
